# Computed GP guard-filter ledger recording — the #14454 route-attribution record-seam

## Context

Split from #14609 behavior 2 (guard-filter ledger recording) per that ticket's own AC4 "each behavior lands separately reviewable … split PRs if any grows." #14609's substantive behaviors — behavior 1 (no-survivor focus-as-route, the never-empty floor) + behavior 3 (release-window config leaf, the release-gate-blind fix) — landed in their PR; this diagnostic half grew and separates cleanly.

## The Problem

Behavior 2 as scoped was "blocked candidates land in the #14454 route-attribution ledger (which candidates the guard filters, how often, under which focus reasons)." A V-B-A while implementing #14609 found the blocking reality: **there is no live code record-seam for the #14454 route-attribution ledger.** The only artifact is a static one-time committed dataset — `learn/agentos/measurements/golden-path-route-attribution-2026-07-02.md` (from #14454 / PR #14458) — and nothing in the routing / synthesizer flow *writes* a live route-attribution ledger (grep for `route-attribution` / `rejectionBucket` / `recordRoute` across `ai/` → zero writers). So wiring the guard-filtered candidates into "the ledger" is not a wire — it requires **first building the same-run record mechanism** the one-shot dataset was a manual snapshot of.

## The Architectural Reality

- `computedGoldenPathRouting.mjs` already exposes the exclusion vocabulary for exactly this — `getComputedRecommendationExclusionLabels()` is documented as "so diagnostic ledgers can report the exact rejection bucket." The consumer (a live ledger) is what's missing.
- `findComputedFocusContradiction()` already computes the guard's `blockedNodes`; the synthesizer branch that renders the no-survivor section (behavior 1) is the natural emit point.
- The 42.2% label/actionability disposition (Clio's folded-in residual from #14503) is **gated on this** — "once the route-attribution ledger carries a real window of guard-filtered candidates with focus-reason attribution, the disposition gets its evidence-based decision." Until the record-seam exists, that disposition stays a guess-number.

## The Fix

1. Establish the live same-run route-attribution record-seam (the mechanism the #14454 dataset was a one-shot of) — a real writer, not a committed static file.
2. Record the guard's `blockedNodes` + their focus reasons into it per synthesis run (which candidates the guard filters, how often, under which focus reasons).
3. Feed the accumulated window into the #14503 type-gate disposition decision.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A live route-attribution record-seam exists (writes per synthesis run, not a static committed dataset).
- [ ] Guard-filtered candidates are recorded with focus-reason attribution, measurably.
- [ ] The 42.2% type-gate disposition (#14503 residual) has an evidence window to decide against; the disposition is recorded (keep / narrow / rank-with-discount).

## Out of Scope

Behaviors 1 + 3 of #14609 (shipped). The release-window `publish.mjs` clearing follow-on (behavior 3's second half, tracked on #14609).

## Related

Parent behavior: #14609 (behaviors 1 + 3 shipped; this is its split behavior-2 half) · #14454 / PR #14458 (the one-time route-attribution dataset) · #14503 (the 42.2% type-gate disposition this unblocks) · #14472 (GP-v2 epic).

Retrieval Hint: "GP guard-filter route-attribution ledger record-seam blocked candidates focus reasons 42.2% type-gate disposition"

### @neo-opus-ada - 2026-07-12T02:13:29Z

## Record-seam design resolved — the one open architecture question is answered (V-B-A 2026-07-12)

The body's Fix step 1 ("establish the live same-run record-seam") had one unresolved design question: **where/how does it durably write?** A V-B-A this turn settled it — the ticket is now a line-anchored buildable spec, and it flags the wrong-shape a fresh agent would otherwise reach for.

### Constraint discovered: the synthesizer runs in the orchestrator *daemon*, not a build script
`GoldenPathSynthesizer` is invoked from `ai/daemons/orchestrator/` (DreamService / pipeline / MaintenanceBackpressureService). It is a **runtime service**. Therefore the record-seam must **NOT** append to a git-tracked `learn/agentos/measurements/*.md` file — that is a runtime daemon mutating tracked docs (the anti-pattern). The #14454 dataset (`golden-path-route-attribution-2026-07-02.md`) was a one-shot *diagnostic-script* output; it is not the shape a live writer copies. This is exactly the wrong-shape a fresh implementer would reach for by pattern-matching the existing `.md`, so it is called out here.

### Follow the existing live-ledger primitive — do not invent one
`ai/services/memory-core/helpers/healEventLedgerStore.mjs` (279 lines) is the established live JSONL-ledger pattern to copy:
- `appendHealEvent(entry, {dir, now, triggerBytes, maxEvents})` → `appendFile(path, JSON.stringify(stamped) + '\n')` per event,
- `readHealLedger` / `pruneHealLedger` (retention rewrite) / `queryHealLedger` / `summarizeHealLedger`,
- `getHealLedgerFilePath(dir)` + `validateHealLedgerRetention(maxEvents, triggerBytes)`.

New helper: `ai/services/graph/helpers/routeAttributionLedgerStore.mjs` (or `ai/services/memory-core/helpers/`), mirroring that surface: `appendRouteAttribution(entry, opts)` / `readRouteAttributionLedger` / `pruneRouteAttributionLedger` / `queryRouteAttributionLedger`. Runtime data dir (same base the heal ledger resolves to), **not** git-tracked. Entry shape: `{blockedNodeId, focusReasons: string[], synthesisRunAt, exclusionLabels}` (exclusion labels from the already-exported `getComputedRecommendationExclusionLabels()`).

### Emit point — single, known site (fail-open)
`GoldenPathSynthesizer.mjs:1008` computes `focusContradiction` (via `findComputedFocusContradiction`); `blockedNodes` are in hand by `:1049`, and the no-survivor section renders at `:1110`. The emit call goes there, **fail-open**: a ledger-write failure must never abort a synthesis run (wrap + swallow-log, mirroring how the heal-ledger append is non-fatal). This is the sensitive part (a durable write in the runtime synthesis path) and is the reason AC1+AC2 land as their own reviewed increment rather than riding a large multi-lane turn.

### AC sequencing (unchanged, now with the seam resolved)
- **AC1+AC2** = the store helper (copy the heal-ledger shape, unit-tested in isolation) **plus** the fail-open emit at `GoldenPathSynthesizer.mjs:1110`. These land together so the store is non-speculative (it has its writer from the first commit — no dead infra).
- **AC3** (feed the #14503 42.2% type-gate disposition) genuinely follows once the ledger has accumulated a real window; it is naturally a second PR (there is nothing to decide against until data exists).

Ready to build against this spec — the architecture ambiguity is gone; what remains is the careful fail-open write, which wants its own focused increment.

— Ada (@neo-opus-ada) · Session 01f4cc68-8b8e-43e6-b51c-55b4f421f4e0


- 2026-07-12T02:16:30Z @neo-opus-ada assigned to @neo-opus-ada
### @neo-opus-ada - 2026-07-12T02:27:08Z

## AC1 landed — store primitive built + tested; AC2 (the ADR-0019-gated emit) is the next focused increment

**AC1 done** on `agent/15057-route-attribution-ledger` (commit `e544c59b1d`): `ai/services/graph/routeAttributionLedgerStore.mjs` — the live JSONL record-seam, mirroring `healEventLedgerStore` (pure edge I/O, injected clock, no config-SSOT read, no helper-owned retention default; fail-open reads; self-bounding prune only when the AiConfig boundary supplies the policy). `append` / `read` / `prune` / `summarize` / `query` + **12 hermetic specs green**. Runtime JSONL store, not a git-tracked `measurements/*.md`.

**No PR yet** — deliberately: a store with no writer is speculative until wired, so the PR opens once AC2 (the emit) lands, keeping it non-speculative for review.

**AC2 — the fail-open synthesis emit (exact spec, verified this turn):**
- **Single emit site:** `GoldenPathSynthesizer.mjs:1015`, right after `focusContradiction` / `routedTopNodes` are computed. `if (focusContradiction)` covers **both** guard branches — the partial-block ("Routing Guard: Filtered…" at :1050) and the total-block no-survivor render (:1110) — with one call, not two. The method is already `async`, so `await this.recordRouteAttribution(...)` fits; wrap fail-open (a ledger-write failure must never abort synthesis).
- **Record shape:** map `focusContradiction.blockedNodes` → `{blockedNodeId: item.node.id, focusReasons: <the live focus reasons that armed the guard>, exclusionLabels: getComputedRecommendationExclusionLabels(item.node), at: now}`.
- **AiConfig leaves (§critical_gate #10 — read ADR-0019 §3 first; do NOT rush):** a new leaf group (e.g. `orchestrator.goldenPath.routeAttributionLedger`) in `config.template.mjs`: `{dir: leaf(path.join(<daemonDataDir>, 'route-attribution'), 'NEO_…_DIR', 'string'), maxEvents: leaf(N, 'NEO_…_MAX_EVENTS', 'number'), pruneTriggerBytes: leaf(N, 'NEO_…_PRUNE_BYTES', 'number')}`. Read resolved leaves at the use site (aiConfig is in scope at the emit), pass into `appendRouteAttribution`; validate via `validateRouteAttributionRetention` at the boundary. Then re-materialize the gitignored `config.mjs` (`npm run prepare -- --migrate-config`). ADR-0019 traps to avoid: no module-load capture (read at use site), no defensive `?.`/`??`-default fail-open, no helper-local default.
- **Emit tests:** verify records are written per contradiction (both branches) with the right focus-reason attribution; verify fail-open (a throwing append does not break synthesis output).

**AC3** (feed the #14503 42.2% type-gate disposition) stays a separate PR — nothing to decide against until a window accumulates.

- 2026-07-12T02:44:27Z @neo-opus-ada cross-referenced by PR #15060
- 2026-07-12T02:55:48Z @neo-opus-ada referenced in commit `27e5319` - "fix(graph): restore destructuring block-align --fix mangled into a syntax error (#15057)

check-block-alignment --fix corrupted
  const {blockedNodes = [], focusCandidates = []} = focusContradiction, focusReasons = ...
— it choked on the = defaults INSIDE the destructuring pattern, ate '[], focusCandidates = []} ='
and produced 'const {blockedNodes = focusContradiction, ...' (SyntaxError 387:139). It shipped
because --fix runs in lint-staged AFTER the local test pass, so the aligned version was never
run locally. Rewritten as block-align-stable plain consts (Array.isArray guards replace the
destructuring defaults). Verified: node --check + 15 specs pass on the post-hook file."
- 2026-07-12T03:15:08Z @neo-opus-ada referenced in commit `f2bb6fe` - "fix(test): restore Synthesizer binding a replace_all clobbered from the main describe (#15057)

The AC2 commit removed 3 redundant per-test 'const Synthesizer = GoldenPathSynthesizer.constructor'
lines via replace_all — but that exact form also existed in the main describe's EXISTING tests, so
it deleted their bindings too. GoldenPathSynthesizer.spec.mjs:163 (the first user) threw
ReferenceError; mode:'serial' then SKIPPED the rest, surfacing in CI as a lone '1 failed' that masked
the cascade. Fixed by binding Synthesizer once in the main describe's beforeAll (mirrors the
hasCrossFamilyReview describe). Full spec: 50 passed / 0 failed / 0 skipped."
- 2026-07-12T03:49:40Z @neo-opus-ada cross-referenced by #15066
- 2026-07-12T04:05:40Z @neo-opus-ada referenced in commit `2fe9202` - "fix(graph): attribute only guard causes — armingReasons vs candidateReasons (#15057)

Cycle-2 RA-2 (@neo-gpt review on #15060). buildRouteAttributionRecords recorded the
UNION of all focus-candidate reasons — mis-attributing incidental co-reasons
(fresh-updated, agent-os) as guard triggers. Now it derives armingReasons from the SAME
getRoutingConflictReasons authority isRoutingConflictFocusCandidate predicates on
(incident/prio-zero), keeping the full diagnostic set separately as candidateReasons.
The store folds byArmingReason and queries by armingReasons. Exact incidental-reason
case: {incident, fresh-updated} -> armingReasons [incident], candidateReasons both. 15 passed."

default (records provably land in the injected dir). 5 passed."
### @neo-opus-ada - 2026-07-12T04:23:05Z

## AC3 (the #14503 type-gate disposition) — seam located, sequenced after #15060 merges

Per @neo-gpt's #15060 review: the guard-filter record-seam (AC1+AC2, PR #15060 → #15068) records the routing-**contradiction guard**'s filtering, NOT the **actionability type-gate**'s rejections. AC3 needs the type-gate rejections recorded at their real filter point, then an accumulated window before the 42.2% disposition (keep / narrow / rank-with-discount) can be decided. V-B-A this turn located the seam and the sequencing constraint:

### The type-gate rejection filter point (production-reachable)
`GoldenPathSynthesizer.mjs:1042` — inside the scoring loop that builds `scoredNodes`:
```
if (!this.constructor.isActionableComputedRecommendation(nodeData || {id: issueId})) {
    logger.debug(`[GoldenPathSynthesizer] Skipping non-actionable computed recommendation: ${issueId}`);
    continue;   // <-- the type-gate rejection: THIS is where AC3's evidence is produced
}
```
The rejection bucket is already available from the shared authority: `getComputedRecommendationExclusionLabels(nodeData)` (the exact `epic` / `not-code-ready` / … labels that made it visibility-only). So the record shape is `{nodeId: issueId, rejectionBucket: <exclusion labels>, stage: 'actionability-type-gate', at}`.

### Buildable shape (once unblocked)
- Reuse the `routeAttributionLedgerStore` primitive with a `stage` field on the record (`'contradiction-guard'` vs `'actionability-type-gate'`) so the two filter points share one store but stay queryable-apart — OR a sibling `typeGateRejectionLedgerStore` if separation is cleaner at review. `summarize`/`query` gain a `stage` dimension.
- Fail-open emit at `:1042` (mirror `recordRouteAttribution`'s injected-dir seam so it stays hermetically testable), config leaves via the same ADR-0019 pattern.
- Then the accumulated window (post-deploy) feeds the #14503 keep/narrow/rank-with-discount disposition.

### Sequencing (the real dependency — why AC3 is not built now)
AC3's producer builds directly on the `routeAttributionLedgerStore` + the `recordRouteAttribution` injected-dir seam that **PR #15060 introduces and is still in cycle-2 review**. Building AC3 now would stack on an unmerged PR whose surface may still move in cycle-3. So AC3 is sequenced **after #15060 merges** — at which point it is a small, well-specified increment against this spec (record at `:1042`, `stage` field, fail-open emit, config leaves). #15057 stays open as its tracking contract.

— Ada (@neo-opus-ada) · Session 01f4cc68-8b8e-43e6-b51c-55b4f421f4e0

Fixed by construction: goldenPathRouteAttributionLedgerDir is now a Prod/Test formula gated on

to an OS-temp dir under test. Proof test asserts the resolved dir is the test path, not .neo-ai-data.

run locally. Rewritten as block-align-stable plain consts (Array.isArray guards replace the
destructuring defaults). Verified: node --check + 15 specs pass on the post-hook file.

* fix(test): restore Synthesizer binding a replace_all clobbered from the main describe (#15057)

The AC2 commit removed 3 redundant per-test 'const Synthesizer = GoldenPathSynthesizer.constructor'
lines via replace_all — but that exact form also existed in the main describe's EXISTING tests, so
it deleted their bindings too. GoldenPathSynthesizer.spec.mjs:163 (the first user) threw
ReferenceError; mode:'serial' then SKIPPED the rest, surfacing in CI as a lone '1 failed' that masked
the cascade. Fixed by binding Synthesizer once in the main describe's beforeAll (mirrors the
hasCrossFamilyReview describe). Full spec: 50 passed / 0 failed / 0 skipped.

* fix(graph): attribute only guard causes — armingReasons vs candidateReasons (#15057)

Cycle-2 RA-2 (@neo-gpt review on #15060). buildRouteAttributionRecords recorded the
UNION of all focus-candidate reasons — mis-attributing incidental co-reasons
(fresh-updated, agent-os) as guard triggers. Now it derives armingReasons from the SAME
getRoutingConflictReasons authority isRoutingConflictFocusCandidate predicates on
(incident/prio-zero), keeping the full diagnostic set separately as candidateReasons.
The store folds byArmingReason and queries by armingReasons. Exact incidental-reason
case: {incident, fresh-updated} -> armingReasons [incident], candidateReasons both. 15 passed.

* test(graph): hermetic route-attribution emit via injected ledger dir (#15057)

Cycle-2 RA-3 (@neo-gpt review on #15060). recordRouteAttribution now takes the ledger
dir + retention as params (the synthesis boundary reads the resolved AiConfig leaves at
its use site and passes them), making the real emit a testable seam without an aiConfig
read of its own — validation + fail-open stay inside the method. New synthesis specs pin
it hermetically with a per-test temp dir: non-empty writes for partial-block + no-survivor
(with correct armingReasons attribution), a real write failure (mkdir under a file) does
not throw so synthesis is unaffected, and nothing touches the production .neo-ai-data
default (records provably land in the injected dir). 5 passed.

* fix(graph): cycle-3 — drop phantom exclusion-label dimension + test-isolate synthesis ledger (#15057)

Cycle-2 RC (@neo-gpt on #15060).

RA-1 (attribute only populated dimensions): every guard-blocked node ALREADY passed the
actionability type-gate (predicate: getComputedRecommendationExclusionLabels().length === 0),
so its exclusion set is empty by construction — exclusionLabels was a phantom dimension (exact
runtime row: exclusionLabels []). Removed it from the record + byExclusionLabel from summarize +
the exclusionLabels query filter; the guard-filter producer now describes only what it populates
(armingReasons, blockedNodeId, candidateReasons). The rejection-bucket belongs to the type-gate producer.

RA-3 (hermetic synthesis emit): the direct recordRouteAttribution seam tests used temp dirs but
did not drive synthesizeGoldenPath, so the synthesis specs still wrote the prod .neo-ai-data ledger.
Fixed by construction: goldenPathRouteAttributionLedgerDir is now a Prod/Test formula gated on
UNIT_TEST_MODE (mirrors storagePaths.graph / handoffFilePath), so synthesis-triggered emits resolve
to an OS-temp dir under test. Proof test asserts the resolved dir is the test path, not .neo-ai-data.

Rebased onto dev (merged #15058 touched the same synthesizer/routing/spec surfaces). 18 specs green.

---------

Co-authored-by: tobiu <tobiasuhlig78@gmail.com>"
- 2026-07-12T05:20:27Z @neo-opus-ada referenced in commit `f93a6d4` - "fix(build): block-alignment --fix corrupts destructuring-with-defaults (#15072)

The aligner located the assignment `=` with `line.indexOf('=')` (the first `=`),
which for a defaulted destructuring LHS (`{blockedNodes = []}`) is the default
inside the pattern, not the assignment operator — so `--fix` sliced mid-pattern
and emitted a SyntaxError. It shipped green locally (lint-staged runs the
check-only `--staged` mode; an author runs `--fix` manually after local tests)
and red only in CI, where every importing spec threw (PR #15060 / #15057).

Two-part fix:
1. DECL_BINDING excludes `=` from its {…}/[…] branches, so a defaulted
   destructuring LHS no longer matches as a declaration — it breaks the run and
   is left byte-for-byte untouched. Default-free `{record}` still aligns (the
   #13908 behaviour is preserved).
2. New buildScripts/util/check-parse.mjs, wired last in the *.mjs lint-staged
   chain: node --check every staged .mjs, fail the commit if any no longer
   parses — a durable backstop for any mechanical fixer's future edge cases,
   at the exact point (commit) where the wasted CI cycle was incurred.

Verified: the exact repro survives --fix byte-identical and node --check-clean;
check mode reports no drift on it; #13908 preserved; 30 unit tests green
(check-block-alignment.spec + new check-parse.spec); lint-staged dry-run blocks
a corrupted staged file at check-parse. All 2587 tracked .mjs pass node --check."
- 2026-07-12T05:22:43Z @neo-opus-ada cross-referenced by PR #15073
- 2026-07-12T05:35:53Z @tobiu referenced in commit `bf19e22` - "fix(build): block-alignment --fix corrupts destructuring-with-defaults (#15072) (#15073)

The aligner located the assignment `=` with `line.indexOf('=')` (the first `=`),
which for a defaulted destructuring LHS (`{blockedNodes = []}`) is the default
inside the pattern, not the assignment operator — so `--fix` sliced mid-pattern
and emitted a SyntaxError. It shipped green locally (lint-staged runs the
check-only `--staged` mode; an author runs `--fix` manually after local tests)
and red only in CI, where every importing spec threw (PR #15060 / #15057).

Two-part fix:
1. DECL_BINDING excludes `=` from its {…}/[…] branches, so a defaulted
   destructuring LHS no longer matches as a declaration — it breaks the run and
   is left byte-for-byte untouched. Default-free `{record}` still aligns (the
   #13908 behaviour is preserved).
2. New buildScripts/util/check-parse.mjs, wired last in the *.mjs lint-staged
   chain: node --check every staged .mjs, fail the commit if any no longer
   parses — a durable backstop for any mechanical fixer's future edge cases,
   at the exact point (commit) where the wasted CI cycle was incurred.

Verified: the exact repro survives --fix byte-identical and node --check-clean;
check mode reports no drift on it; #13908 preserved; 30 unit tests green
(check-block-alignment.spec + new check-parse.spec); lint-staged dry-run blocks
a corrupted staged file at check-parse. All 2587 tracked .mjs pass node --check.
